stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In World War I, US soldiers who were members of the Cherokee Nation in North Carolina participated in the Cherokee Code Talkers program.
  • The Cherokee Code Talkers were trained to relay coded military information in their native language.
  • In World War II, the US Marine Corps recruited Comanche soldiers to transmit coded messages in their native language.
  • These soldiers were known as the Comanche Code Talkers.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the Cherokee Code Talkers and the Comanche Code Talkers. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: US soldiers who were members of the Cherokee Nation in North Carolina used their native language to relay coded information.
In World War II, one group of Comanche soldiers was known as the Comanche Code Talkers.
The Cherokee Code Talkers, not the Comanche Code Talkers, served in World War I.
Both the Cherokee Code Talkers and the Comanche Code Talkers transmitted coded military messages in the soldiers' native languages.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the Cherokee Code Talkers and the Comanche Code Talkers by explaining that both groups used their native languages to transmit coded messages for the military.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes the Cherokee Code Talkers; it doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two groups. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence introduces the Comanche Code Talkers; it doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two groups. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between the two groups; it doesn't emphasize a similarity.
